data.narrative:
Oxford Nanopore Technologies is a public-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 public peers at equivalent maturity: Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.6B), Freenome ($1.1B), and Alto Neuroscience ($1.0B).

A peer-median method was applied across these 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables, yielding a median implied valuation of $1.1B with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.6B. A public-comparable anchoring approach using sector price-to-sales multiples (median 6.9x across Biotech & Health tickers RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) was not applicable, as the target's revenue was not available for analysis—a limitation typical of private company disclosure.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $935M (low), $1.1B (median), to $1.3B (high). Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data, which prevents multiples-based validation; limited peer comparability given the heterogeneity of biotech business models; and market-dependent factors affecting public biotech valuations. The median estimate carries moderate confidence given the tight clustering of peer valuations around $1.0B–$1.1B, though a second independent method would materially strengthen conviction in the range.
